results.identified_issue: Interpreted 'the buttons' as the question banner + its chevron toggle on the Discover feed, which is the only screen in the app (RootView shows DiscoverView). The banner was a VStack sibling of the feed, so showing/hiding it (or switching to a longer/shorter question) resized the ScrollView and moved every full-bleed video page. Fix: overlay the banner (and the transient download note) on the feed instead. If 'all the buttons' meant specific controls I couldn't find (the toolbar's settings/browse buttons are already fixed in the nav bar and don't move), point me at them and I'll pull those into the overlay too.
results.detailed_description: You're right that the feed is already a full-screen stack, so nothing should move when the question expands or contracts. The question banner was living in a VStack directly above the feed, which meant tapping the chevron to hide/show it — or just landing on a question that wraps to more lines — resized the scroll view and pushed every video page down or up. I moved the banner so it floats as an overlay pinned to the top of the feed, over an ultraThinMaterial background so the text stays readable over whatever frame is playing. The video underneath now keeps the whole screen and stays put; toggling the question just fades the banner in and out in place (I animated the chevron and gave the banner a move/opacity transition). The toolbar buttons (settings, the chevron, browse) were already fixed in the navigation bar and never moved, so I left those as they are. Not build-verified — I don't build from this flow — so this needs a look on device to confirm the banner reads well over both the intro card and an argument clip.
results.summary: Floated the question banner over the feed so toggling it no longer shoves the video down

description: All the buttons should just be overlays so that when we expand or contract the question it shouldn't move anything down because it's just in a stack anyway.
